raw_title: Symbolic Custodians on Facebook: Community Media Agents and Religious Practices in Faial
raw_abstract: The digital mediatization of social life constitutes a pervasive process that extends – not unambiguously – to small, grassroots communities. On Faial Island, in the Portuguese archipelago of the Azores, an informal group of non-professional photographers and videographers have integrated Facebook into their documentation of the island’s most cherished cultural and spiritual capital: the Holy Spirit and the patronal festivals. This practice has evolved into a modern custodianship of cultural and religious symbolism and memory. Drawing upon field and digital ethnographies combined with eight semi-structured interviews, this article examines how their practice has been integrated into the cultural and religious repertoire of Faial’s peri-rural communities, mediatizing representation, communication, memorialization, and access. Findings seem to indicate that these hyperlocal media agents provide valuable services to their communities, both locally and among the diaspora, through: (1) promotion of the island’s religious and cultural heritage; (2) virtual access to celebrations for those unable to attend physically; and (3) creation of material testimonies for future memory. Simultaneously, their practice seems to raise questions regarding reliability and ownership, posing new challenges to communities’ symbolic agency. Results demonstrate how Facebook integration into religious communitarian practices simultaneously promotes cultural resistance and pervasive cultural de-singularization.
title_en: Symbolic Custodians on Facebook: Community Media Agents and Religious Practices in Faial
title_zh: Facebook上的象征性守护者：法亚尔岛的社区媒体代理与宗教实践
abstract_en: The digital mediatization of social life constitutes a pervasive process that extends – not unambiguously – to small, grassroots communities. On Faial Island, in the Portuguese archipelago of the Azores, an informal group of non-professional photographers and videographers have integrated Facebook into their documentation of the island’s most cherished cultural and spiritual capital: the Holy Spirit and the patronal festivals. This practice has evolved into a modern custodianship of cultural and religious symbolism and memory. Drawing upon field and digital ethnographies combined with eight semi-structured interviews, this article examines how their practice has been integrated into the cultural and religious repertoire of Faial’s peri-rural communities, mediatizing representation, communication, memorialization, and access. Findings seem to indicate that these hyperlocal media agents provide valuable services to their communities, both locally and among the diaspora, through: (1) promotion of the island’s religious and cultural heritage; (2) virtual access to celebrations for those unable to attend physically; and (3) creation of material testimonies for future memory. Simultaneously, their practice seems to raise questions regarding reliability and ownership, posing new challenges to communities’ symbolic agency. Results demonstrate how Facebook integration into religious communitarian practices simultaneously promotes cultural resistance and pervasive cultural de-singularization.
abstract_zh: 社会生活的数字媒介化是一个普遍的过程，它——并非毫无歧义地——延伸到小型草根社区。在葡萄牙亚速尔群岛的法亚尔岛，一个由非专业摄影师和摄像师组成的非正式团体将Facebook融入他们对岛上最珍视的文化和精神资本——圣灵和守护神节庆——的记录中。这种做法已演变为对文化和宗教象征及记忆的现代守护。基于田野和数字民族志以及八次半结构化访谈，本文考察了他们的实践如何被整合到法亚尔岛半乡村社区的文化和宗教库中，媒介化表征、沟通、纪念和访问。研究结果表明，这些超本地媒体代理为其社区（包括本地和散居群体）提供了有价值的服务，通过：（1）推广岛上的宗教和文化遗产；（2）为无法亲自参加的人提供虚拟访问庆祝活动；（3）为未来记忆创造物质见证。同时，他们的实践似乎引发了关于可靠性和所有权的问题，对社区的象征性代理提出了新的挑战。结果展示了Facebook融入宗教社区实践如何同时促进文化抵抗和普遍的文化去特殊化。
plain_summary_zh: 这项研究探讨了在葡萄牙亚速尔群岛的法亚尔岛上，一群非专业的摄影师和摄像师如何利用Facebook记录和分享当地的宗教节日（如圣灵节和守护神节）。他们成为了这些文化传统的现代守护者，帮助推广遗产、让无法到场的人虚拟参与，并为后代留下记录。但这也引发了关于信息可靠性和所有权的问题。研究表明，Facebook的使用既有助于保护当地文化，也可能导致文化特色的减弱。
plain_summary_en: This study looks at how a group of non-professional photographers and videographers on Faial Island in the Azores, Portugal, use Facebook to document and share local religious festivals (like the Holy Spirit and patron saint celebrations). They have become modern guardians of these cultural traditions, helping to promote heritage, allow virtual participation for those who can't attend, and create records for future generations. However, this also raises questions about reliability and ownership. The research shows that using Facebook both helps preserve local culture and may lead to a loss of cultural uniqueness.
research_direction_zh: 社交媒体在地方社区宗教和文化实践中的角色与影响
research_direction_en: The role and impact of social media in local community religious and cultural practices
